executiveSummary:
# Medicaid Program Performance Executive Summary

National Medicaid enrollment stands at 74.9 million beneficiaries as of February 2026, with all major states experiencing year-over-year declines following the end of pandemic-era continuous enrollment protections. California leads with 12.4 million enrollees despite a notable 6.8% decrease, while Texas maintains 4.0 million enrollees with a 3.8% decline that tracks closely with peer states Florida (-4.2%) and Georgia (-3.9%). Michigan experienced the steepest enrollment drop at 7.6%, while North Carolina achieved enrollment stability with no year-over-year change. Total program expenditures reached $215.9 billion in fiscal year 2024, with a national average per-enrollee cost of $2,883. Texas demonstrates relatively efficient spending at $3,014 per enrollee compared to higher-cost states like Pennsylvania ($4,363) and Massachusetts ($4,221), though it exceeds more efficient programs in Florida ($2,036) and North Carolina ($2,623).

Quality performance remains highly variable across states, with a national average score of 65.6 out of 100 points. Massachusetts leads at 81 points while Mississippi ranks lowest at 48 points, creating a concerning 33-point performance gap. Texas scores 62 points, ranking 30th nationally in the moderate performance tier, indicating significant room for improvement compared to top-performing states. The state's managed care penetration of 93.3% exceeds the national average of 83.2%, suggesting strong infrastructure for coordinated care delivery. Federal regulatory activity remains robust with multiple proposed rules addressing community engagement requirements, state directed payments, and provider enrollment moratoria that may impact state program administration and costs in the coming fiscal year.
texasSpotlight:
## Texas Medicaid Program Analysis

**Enrollment and Cost Efficiency Show Mixed Performance**
Texas operates the nation's third-largest Medicaid program with 4.0 million enrollees, representing a manageable -3.8% year-over-year decline that tracks closely with national post-pandemic normalization trends and falls between peer states Florida (-4.2%) and North Carolina (0.0%). The state demonstrates notable cost discipline with per-enrollee expenditures of $3,014, running 4.6% above the national average of $2,883 but significantly below expansion states like Pennsylvania ($4,363) and Ohio ($3,584). Among large non-expansion states, Texas sits between Florida's ultra-lean $2,036 per enrollee and what would be expected for a state of its complexity. Total program expenditures of $12.0 billion position Texas as the fourth-largest Medicaid spender nationally, though this reflects the state's large eligible population rather than inefficient spending patterns.

**Quality and Delivery Model Present Strategic Opportunities**
Texas faces meaningful quality challenges with a score of 62/100, ranking 30th nationally and trailing southern peers like North Carolina while sitting well below the national average of 65.6. This moderate performance suggests significant room for improvement, particularly given that only 10 states achieve high-performer status (≥75). However, the state's managed care infrastructure provides a strong foundation for quality improvements, with 93.3% penetration rate substantially exceeding the 83.2% national average and covering 3.86 million enrollees. This robust managed care platform, combined with active federal regulatory attention to state-directed payments and community engagement requirements visible in recent Federal Register activity, positions Texas to leverage its delivery system scale for better outcomes. The state should particularly focus on care coordination and population health initiatives that could move it from the moderate tier toward higher performance while maintaining its cost efficiency advantages.

alerts.title: Pennsylvania Per-Enrollee Spending 51% Above National Average
alerts.description: Pennsylvania's per-enrollee spending of $4,363 is significantly higher than the national average of $2,883, indicating potential cost control issues.

alerts.title: Massachusetts High Spending Despite Top Quality Performance
alerts.description: Massachusetts has the highest per-enrollee spending at $4,221 while achieving top quality scores (81/100), suggesting high-cost but effective care delivery.

alerts.title: Florida Extremely Low Per-Enrollee Spending Raises Quality Concerns
alerts.description: Florida's per-enrollee spending of $2,036 is 29% below national average despite having the 4th largest enrollment, potentially indicating underfunding.

alerts.title: Five States Show Critical Quality Performance Below 55
alerts.description: Arkansas, South Carolina, Alabama, Oklahoma, and Mississippi all scored below 55/100 on quality measures, well below the national average of 65.6.

alerts.title: Federal Enrollment Moratorium Active Across All Programs
alerts.description: Recent Federal Register notices indicate temporary enrollment moratoria are in effect for Medicare, Medicaid, and CHIP programs nationwide.

executiveInsights.title: Texas Medicaid enrollment declining -3.8% YoY with 3.99M enrollees
executiveInsights.summary: Texas Medicaid enrollment dropped to 3,997,752 enrollees, representing a -3.8% year-over-year decline. This places Texas as the 3rd largest Medicaid program nationally but experiencing notable enrollment contraction.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Declining enrollment could signal upcoming budget pressures for HHSC and potential contract scope adjustments. Accenture should prepare for possible service volume reductions or pivot strategies.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Flag enrollment decline trends to HHSC leadership and review contract volume assumptions

executiveInsights.title: Texas quality score at 62/100 ranks #30 nationally, below average
executiveInsights.summary: Texas Medicaid quality performance scores 62/100, ranking 30th out of 50 states and falling below the national average of 65.6. The state is categorized as 'moderate' performer, not in the bottom tier but significantly behind top performers.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Poor quality rankings create regulatory risk and potential federal oversight pressure. This represents a clear improvement opportunity that Accenture should prioritize to demonstrate value to HHSC.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Review quality improvement initiatives and propose targeted interventions to HHSC

executiveInsights.title: Texas managed care penetration at 93.3% exceeds national average of 83.2%
executiveInsights.summary: Texas has achieved 93.3% managed care penetration with 3,863,808 enrollees, significantly outperforming the national average of 83.2%. This represents strong managed care adoption in the state.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: High managed care penetration indicates Texas is well-positioned for value-based care initiatives and population health management. Accenture should leverage this strength in proposing advanced analytics and care coordination solutions.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Develop managed care optimization proposals leveraging Texas's high penetration rate

executiveInsights.title: Multiple CMS enrollment moratoria announced affecting Medicaid programs
executiveInsights.summary: Federal Register shows multiple recent announcements of nationwide temporary moratoria on enrollment for Medicare, Medicaid, and CHIP programs. Two separate moratoria were announced on May 15, 2026.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Federal enrollment restrictions could significantly impact Texas Medicaid operations and require immediate policy response. HHSC will need guidance on implementation and compliance requirements.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Flag CMS enrollment moratoria to HHSC policy team for immediate compliance review

executiveInsights.title: Texas per-enrollee costs at $3,014 align closely with national average
executiveInsights.summary: Texas Medicaid spending averages $3,014 per enrollee compared to the national average of $2,883. With total expenditures of $12.05 billion, Texas maintains relatively controlled per-capita costs despite being the 4th largest program by spending.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Cost alignment with national benchmarks suggests current financial management is reasonable, but provides limited differentiation opportunity. Accenture should focus on quality improvements rather than pure cost reduction strategies.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Review cost optimization opportunities beyond per-capita spending reduction

riskOpportunity.label: Enrollment decline acceleration
riskOpportunity.description: Texas Medicaid enrollment dropping 3.8% YoY could accelerate, reducing program scale and contract value.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Develop enrollment stabilization analytics and retention strategies for HHSC.

riskOpportunity.label: Quality improvement mandate
riskOpportunity.description: Texas ranks 30th nationally in quality measures, creating pressure for systematic improvements.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Propose comprehensive quality analytics platform to elevate Texas from moderate to high performer tier.

riskOpportunity.label: Managed care recompete
riskOpportunity.description: High MC penetration of 93.3% suggests potential MCO contract renewals worth billions in oversight.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Position for MCO oversight and integration services during upcoming recompetition cycles.

riskOpportunity.label: Federal regulatory tsunami
riskOpportunity.description: Multiple CMS regulatory changes in 2026 create compliance complexity requiring system updates.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Establish dedicated regulatory compliance team to manage CMS rule implementation for Texas.

riskOpportunity.label: Cost efficiency optimization
riskOpportunity.description: Texas per-enrollee costs of $3,014 are above national average, creating pressure for cost management.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Deploy AI-driven cost optimization tools to reduce per-enrollee expenditures below national average.

riskOpportunity.label: OIG audit exposure
riskOpportunity.description: Active HHS OIG oversight of Medicaid programs increases audit risk for Texas operations.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Strengthen audit readiness protocols and compliance monitoring across all Texas HHSC contracts.

riskOpportunity.label: Digital transformation acceleration
riskOpportunity.description: Provider enrollment moratorium signals need for enhanced digital systems and process automation.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Propose next-generation digital platform to modernize Texas Medicaid provider and enrollment systems.

riskOpportunity.label: Competitor market entry
riskOpportunity.description: Active procurement opportunities in Texas create openings for competitors to challenge Accenture's position.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Monitor Texas ESBD daily and pre-position for strategic procurement opportunities before RFP release.

pulseMetrics.tooltip: Texas has 3,997,752 Medicaid enrollees as of February 2026.

pulseMetrics.tooltip: Texas spent $3,014 per Medicaid enrollee in fiscal year 2024.

pulseMetrics.label: Quality Score
pulseMetrics.tooltip: Texas ranks #30 of 50 states with a moderate quality score of 62 out of 100 in 2024.

pulseMetrics.label: Active Procurement Items
pulseMetrics.tooltip: Two Texas-specific procurement opportunities are currently tracked across HHSC and ESBD portals.

pulseMetrics.tooltip: Eleven federal regulatory and OIG items are currently active across Medicaid programs.

pulseMetrics.label: Next Milestone
pulseMetrics.tooltip: No specific upcoming milestones identified in the available data.
